author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Part of the Proceedings of the Council of Carthage Against Cœlestius.
title: Part of the Proceedings of the Council of Carthage Against Cœlestius.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 3 [III.]—Part of the Proceedings of the Council of Carthage Against Cœlestius.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: “The bishop Aurelius said: ‘Let what follows be recited.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It was accordingly recited, ‘That the sin of Adam was injurious to him alone, and not to the human race.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Then, after the recital, Cœlestius said: ‘I said that I was in doubt about the transmission of sin, but so as to yield assent to any man whom God has gifted with the grace of knowledge; for I have heard different opinions from those who have been even appointed presbyters in the Catholic Church.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The deacon Paulinus said: ‘Tell us their names.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Cœlestius answered: ‘The holy presbyter Rufinus, who lived at Rome with the holy Pammachius.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: I have heard him declare that there is no transmission of sin.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The deacon Paulinus then asked: ‘Is there any one else?’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Cœlestius replied: ‘I have heard more say the same.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The deacon Paulinus rejoined: ‘Tell us their names.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Cœlestius said: ‘Is not one priest enough for you?’”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Then afterwards in another place we read: “The bishop Aurelius said: ‘Let the rest of the accusation be read.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It then was recited ‘That infants at their birth are in the same state that Adam was before the transgression;’ and they read to the very end of the brief accusation which had been previously put in.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The bishop Aurelius inquired: ‘Have you, Cœlestius, taught at any time, as the deacon Paulinus has stated, that infants are at their birth in the same state that Adam was before his transgression?’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Cœlestius answered: ‘Let him explain what he meant when he said, “before the transgression.’”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The deacon Paulinus then said: ‘Do you on your side deny that you ever taught this doctrine?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: It must be one of two things: he must either say that he never so taught, or else he must now condemn the opinion.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Cœlestius rejoined: ‘I have already said, Let him explain the words he mentioned, “before the transgression.”’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The deacon Paulinus then said: ‘You must deny ever having taught this.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The bishop Aurelius said: ‘I ask, What conclusion I have on my part to draw from this man’s obstinacy; my affirmation is, that although Adam, as created in Paradise, is said to have been made immortal at first, he afterwards became corruptible through transgressing the commandment.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Do you say this, brother Paulinus?’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: ‘I do, my lord,’ answered the deacon Paulinus.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Then the bishop Aurelius said: ‘As regards the condition of infants before baptism at the present day, the deacon Paulinus wishes to be informed whether it is such as Adam’s was before the transgression; and whether it derives the guilt of transgression from the same origin of sin from which it is born?’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The deacon Paulinus asked: ‘Let him deny whether he taught this, or not.’

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Cœlestius answered: ‘As touching the transmission of sin, I have already asserted, that I have heard many persons of acknowledged position in the catholic Church deny it altogether; and on the other hand, others affirm it: it may be fairly deemed a matter for inquiry, but not a heresy.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: I have always maintained that infants require baptism, and ought to be baptized.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: What else does he want?’”
